behavior_summary: U8P_BIGWEBNS::gotHit is a short process that immediately removes the object instance when it receives a hit/damage event.
Gameplay effect: the entity (inferred to be a web) vanishes when struck, with no branching, dialogue, or state changes.
The routine accepts two parameters (uword, word) from the engine but contains a single call to Item::destroy(this) and does not use the parameters.
notable_control_flow: Single-path: on entry the process invokes Item::destroy(this) and then terminates; there are no conditionals or loops.
No global flags are read or written; no menus or player-facing text are involved.
open_questions: Why the declared parameters (uword, word) exist if they are unused — are they engine-provided hit metadata supplied to all hit handlers?
Does destroying this instance trigger any additional engine-side effects (particles, score, or quest checks) not visible in this script?
Is this behavior intended for temporary/fragile obstacles (e.g., webs) only, or used by multiple object types that should vanish on impact?

main_function.name: U8P_BIGWEBNS::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle a hit/damage event by destroying the current item instance so it disappears from the game world.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the object receives a hit/damage event (process type 0x020C).
Receives two parameters (uword, word) from the engine, likely hit-related data (inferred).

function_map.name: U8P_BIGWEBNS::gotHit
function_map.role: destroy-on-hit handler for the object instance

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: engine/item API to remove/destroy an item instance from the world

external_calls.purpose: Remove/destroy the current item instance (cleanup/removal from map).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_BIGWEBNS::gotHit is a short process that immediately removes the object instance when it receives a hit/damage event.
